_comment: EXAMPLE job input only — not part of the engine. Shows the shape the orchestrator/web app feeds roar_render.render() in production: a voice_id, an ordered list of narration lines, and optional recipe overrides. Any user's instrumental + story produces a file like this. Reproduces the approved money-shot render. CLI: python scripts/roar_render.py INSTRUMENTAL OUT.mp3 scripts/sample_job.json
lines: Sit down. Let an old voice tell you the truth you already carry.
Forty-three years. A good man. A worn man. You buried your fire under bills, and routine, and the weight of being needed.
But ash still remembers the flame. And something in you never stopped wanting to build.
Your hardest war was never against other people. It was against the man you used to be.
At twenty-four, they locked a door behind you. Months alone. No distractions. No excuses.
Most men break in there. You rebuilt. You read. You trained your mind. You came out sharper, calmer, wiser.
For one clear moment, you met the man you could become. Then the years stole him back, and the warrior fell asleep.
So wake him. Not the angry boy. The man. Twenty-four's fire, with forty-three's wisdom.
They are not your burden. They are your reason.
They will not remember your speeches. They will remember their father standing back up.
So build. Not walls. Not towers. Build with your mind what your hands already understand.
Thirteen years in the trade, meeting the tools of tomorrow. A builder, becoming a builder again.
You have buried enough versions of yourself to know which one deserves to live.
You were never chasing a crown. You were growing the shoulders to carry its weight.
The world does not need the loudest man in the room. It needs the one who kept standing when no one was watching.
The old chapter is ash. Now, build.
